subject: [PATCH v2 032/110] hfsplus: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of PRIino format for i_ino in hfsplus, specifically that it may not be compatible with older kernels. The author responded by stating that they are aware of this issue and will add a compatibility check to ensure that the PRIino format is only used on newer kernels.

reviews.author: Viacheslav Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not include Reviewed-by tag, which is a required field for kernel patches.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>

Thanks,
Slava.

_______________________________________________
Linux-f2fs-devel mailing list
Linux-f2fs-devel@lists.sourceforge.net
https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the HFS+ file system to use the PRIino format for printing inode numbers, which will be widened in a future change. The update corrects signed format specifiers to unsigned, as inode numbers are unsigned values. The changes affect various files within the hfsplus directory, including attributes.c, catalog.c, dir.c, extents.c, inode.c, super.c, and xattr.c.
